title: Children's Out of Home Respite Care - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Children's Out of Home Respite Care 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disabilities Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness AUTISM SOCIETY OF OREGON AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER INFORMATION AND RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on autism resources, respite evenings for caregivers (“Take a Break on ASO”), and autism-friendly community events. 9320 SW Barbur Boulevard Suite 240 Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Office: Friday 11am-2pm or by appointment Email (503) 636-1676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers comprehensive family support for families with children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Services include therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, family support and individualized case management, home visiting, parenting education, and group activities designed to strengthen parenting skills and promote healthy child development 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Young children ages 0-5 who are experiencing high levels of stress or who have experienced trauma and/or are at risk for abuse and neglect; parents who have multiple risk factors and are struggling to nurture and care for their children. High-risk families typically experience several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parenthood, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, history with drugs and alcohol, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 720 N 14th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY NORTH DOUGLAS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 239 East B Avenue Drain, OR 97435 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY SOUTH LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 75 S 4th street Creswell, OR 97426 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION GRANTS PASS SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Network of local agencies and churches committed to preventing child abuse and helping at-risk families manage crisis situations by providing out-of-home respite care. Volunteer families build relationships with at-risk families and can open their homes to at-risk children. The program works towards child welfare deflection by providing a safe alternative to child welfare custody. Also works towards child abuse prevention by providing an at-risk parent with a safe, temporary place for their child without threat of losing custody and averting potential abuse/neglect episodes. 1504 NE 9th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Ages 0-22. Families eligible if they do not currently have an open case with ODHS Child Welfare. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (541) 956-4190 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Network of local agencies and churches committed to preventing child abuse and helping at-risk families manage crisis situations by providing out-of-home respite care. Volunteer families build relationships with at-risk families and can open their homes to at-risk children. The program works towards child welfare deflection by providing a safe alternative to child welfare custody. Also works towards child abuse prevention by providing an at-risk parent with a safe, temporary place for their child without threat of losing custody and averting potential abuse/neglect episodes. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Ages 0-22. Families eligible if they do not currently have an open case with ODHS Child Welfare. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW GLADSTONE CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nurseries serve families with young children who are most at risk of abuse and neglect. They provide high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. LifeWorks NW provides wraparound services to high-risk families. Services include early childhood intervention and social, emotional, and family support. Also provides mental health services and trauma assessments, traumatic stress expertise, home visitations, parent/infant and parent/toddler classes, art and music therapy, respite child care, and weekly parenting classes using the "Making Parenting a Pleasure" curriculum. Parents/children must be enrolled to receive most services. 18907 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Caregivers with children ages 0-5 and women in their 3rd trimester of pregnancy Serves high-risk families. "High-risk" typically means experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent households, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 283-4776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST YAMHILL COUNTY SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a network of volunteers to offer support to socially isolated families who lack natural support systems. Parents may be provided friendship, mentoring, or short-term respite care for their children during times of crisis without fear of losing custody of their children. Volunteers are fully vetted, trained, and provided professional support. 435 NE Evans Street Suite A McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Families with children or youth adults ages 0-22 who do not currently have an open case with ODHS Child Welfare Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-4pm Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PLANNED AND RESPITE CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short term respite services. Recruits and certifies respite providers for the program. 11035 NE Sandy Boulevard Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: To be a resource respite certified home, the following basic requirements must be met: -At least 21 years old -Can pass a background check -Can demonstrate financial independence (families cannot be dependent upon the foster care stipend) -Complete orientation and pre-certification training, and maintain certification through continued education and training -Reliable transportation -At least one driver over the age of 21 with a valid Oregon driver’s license -Separate bedroom with a door and a window for the foster kid placed in your home Requirements for youth to be placed in a respite home are as follows: -Ages 3-17 -Reside in Multnomah, Clackamas, and Wasington Counties -Active case, respite included in the treatment plan, and referred by the mental health therapist -Have Oregon Health Plan (OHP) coverage (not open card) -Approved by CareOregon with a respite authorization -At risk for out-of-home placement due to their mental health diagnosis -Have behaviors that the service can stabilize -The emotional distress of the family, caused by the youth’s mental health disorder, can be decreased with the service -Not actively suicidal, homicidal, or aggressive -Actively involved with a mental health provider who will provide after-hours crisis intervention during the respite period -Have a placement in a foster home or their family home is available at the end of the respite stay Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (855) 838-4113 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOUNTAINSTAR FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides intensive support through individualized case management, parenting education, home visits, and group activities. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. Preschool Promise offered at all locations except for La Pine. 2125 NE Daggett Lane Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: -Services: Ages 6 weeks-3 years; children of high-risk families -Parent and children must be enrolled to receive most services -Preschool offered Fall 2026 "High-risk" typically means households experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent households, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, history of drug or alcohol use, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: By appointment Email (541) 907-1987 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nursery providing services for at-risk families that include therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers, home visiting, outreach and parent support groups. Resource closet available with food, children's clothing, diapers, wipes, hygiene items and cleaning supplies. 1650 SW 45th Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who are low-income, a single-parent, teen parent, experiencing mental health or drug/alcohol issues, victims of trauma or domestic violence, involvement with child welfare, homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 757-8068 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PIONEER RELIEF NURSERY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides family support, referrals, advocacy, respite care, and parenting classes to parents who are experiencing high levels of stress. Therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers and home visits provided as well. 1312 SW 2nd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Parents experiencing stress with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 215-1017 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON PROVIDENCE CHILD CENTER CENTER FOR MEDICALLY FRAGILE CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 24-hour nursing care for children, including short-term stays, medical evaluation and treatment, recovery after surgery or illness, and respite care. Also offers long-term care with team-based care planning and coordination. 830 NE 47th Avenue Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Age 21 and younger with complex medical needs requiring 24-hour skilled nursing care Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Phone: Monday-Friday 7am-5:30pm (503) 215-2837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides temporary, short-term host homes through a network of vetted volunteer families, allowing parents in crisis to safely place their children while they receive support to address challenging situations without fear of custody loss 12520 SW 3rd Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Cannot host children with signficant medical or behavioral needs Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-9pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 906-1027 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TREASURE VALLEY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with children who are at risk of abuse and neglect by providing intensive support through individualized case management. Also provides parenting education, home visits, developmental assessments, group activities, and referrals to other community resources. Mental health care, counseling for domestic violence, and support for adults recovering from addiction are offered as well. 780 SE 6th Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: -High-risk families with children ages 0-5. Families with children younger than 18 months are served through home visits only -High-risk typically means families experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent household, criminal history, adult survivor of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Classroom sessions: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday. No classes most major holidays including Christmas break. Email (541) 823-2526 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY EAST PORTLAND FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting education, support through home visits, and group activities and intensive case management. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 2609 SE 136th Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: High risk families with children ages 6 weeks to 5 years old. Intakes are completed to determine eligibility. Hours: Therapeutic Early Childhood Program: Monday-Thursday 9:30am-12:30pm Parent Education Classes: Offered quarterly one afternoon a week Respite Care: Monday and Wednesday afternoon for home based and waitlisted families Email (503) 972-9643 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AUTISM SOCIETY OF OREGON AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER INFORMATION AND RESOURCES FAMILY NURTURING CENTER RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY LANE COUNTY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY NORTH DOUGLAS COUNTY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY SOUTH LANE COUNTY HEARTS WITH A MISSION SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN HEARTS WITH A MISSION SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN LIFEWORKS NW CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY LIFEWORKS NW CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY LIFEWORKS NW CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PLANNED AND RESPITE CARE SERVICES MOUNTAINSTAR FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY MOUNTAINSTAR FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY MOUNTAINSTAR FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY MOUNTAINSTAR FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY PIONEER RELIEF NURSERY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY SERVICES PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON CENTER FOR MEDICALLY FRAGILE CHILDREN SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN SAFE FAMILIES FOR CHILDREN TREASURE VALLEY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY
